'''Jordan Tansey''' (born 9 September 1986) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er.

He has previously played for [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], the [[York City Knights]] in the [[Co-operative Championship]] and the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[NRL]].<ref name="YCKSJT"/> Tansey has also played temporarily for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and on a permanent basis for [[RFL League 1|Betfred League 1]] side [[Workington Town]].

He is perhaps best known for scoring the decisive try in [[Leeds Rhinos]]' [[Magic Weekend|Millennium Magic 2007]] match against the [[Bradford Bulls]], when he followed up a [[Kevin Sinfield]] penalty in the last play of the game which hit the crossbar; had the penalty gone over Leeds would only have drawn 38-38.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6625925.stm|work=BBC News|first=Phil|last=Harlow|title=Millennium Magic day two as it happened|date=2007-05-06}}</ref>

==Early life==
As a child Tansey lived in Whinmoor, Leeds. He attended [[Boston Spa Comprehensive School]] from 1998-2002, where he played for the school football team as a striker. Fellow pro Rugby League player [[Danny Williams (rugby)|Danny Williams]] also attended Boston Spa school and played for the same football team as Tansey. The two would eventually transition to Rugby despite Boston Spa School not having an actual Rugby team.

==Sydney Roosters==
On 1 October 2008, Tansey agreed to a deal with Australian [[NRL]] side Sydney Roosters for two years. He was meant to rejoin the [[Leeds Rhinos]] but instead, on 4 June 2009, Tansey signed with the [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] for the 2010 season.<ref name="Tansey loaned to Roosters">{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_4230831,00.html|title=Tansey loaned to Roosters|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-10-01|access-date=2008-10-02}}</ref>

== Return to Super League ==
He returned to England with the [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] rugby league club after being granted an early release from the [[Sydney Roosters]] against their win over the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086400 |title=Engage Super League |access-date=19 July 2009 |archive-date=20 July 2009 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090720091911/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086400 |url-status=dead }}</ref> His contract was terminated following a disciplinary hearing in February 2011.<ref>[http://www.hullfc.com/hullfc/contents/?p=7330] {{dead link|date=April 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> He subsequently joined [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]]. Tansey signed a one-year contract with [[York City Knights]] in January 2012.<ref name=YCKSJT>{{cite news|title=YCKSJT|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16449700|work=BBC}}</ref> He then joined [[Castleford Tigers]] in 2013, playing for two years before being transferred to [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] mid-season in 2015.

In 2018, he had trials with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]].
